title: Patchwork Recipe
description: YAML recipe schema for Patchwork automation

name.description: Recipe name (kebab-case, 1-64 chars). Scoped names like @scope/name are accepted for registry recipes.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this recipe does

maxConcurrency.description: Maximum number of chained steps that may execute in parallel

maxDepth.description: Maximum nested recipe depth for chained recipes

filter.description: File filter pattern (for file_watch trigger). For on_test_run triggers, one of: any | failure | pass-after-fail.

vars.description: Declared input variables resolvable as {{name}} in steps. Place declared variables here (NOT at recipe root — top-level vars are ignored at runtime).

name.description: Variable name (letter/underscore start; letters, digits, underscores; max 64 chars).

required.description: Whether the variable must be supplied.

inputs.description: Alias for trigger.vars — declared input variables resolvable as {{name}} in steps.

name.description: Variable name (letter/underscore start; letters, digits, underscores; max 64 chars).

required.description: Whether the variable must be supplied.

eventSource.description: Webhook/event source name after legacy trigger normalization

eventFilter.description: Webhook/event filter after legacy trigger normalization

eventLeadTimeHours.description: Lead time in hours after legacy trigger normalization

eventLeadTimeMinutes.description: Lead time in minutes after legacy trigger normalization

legacyType.description: Original trigger type preserved during legacy normalization

context.description: Context blocks to load before running steps

id.description: Unique chained step identifier used for outputs and dependencies

awaits.description: Step IDs that must complete before this step runs

when.description: Template condition that controls whether this step runs

optional.description: Whether step failure should be tolerated

transform.description: Template rendered after tool execution. Use $result to reference the tool output. Supports all template expressions.

retry.description: Number of times to retry this step on failure (overrides recipe-level on_error.retry)

timeout_ms.description: Wall-clock timeout in milliseconds. If the step takes longer, the run halts with category step_timeout. Agent steps are not subject to this timeout.

expect.description: Per-step assertions evaluated against the step output. Multiple fields are AND-composed.

schema.description: JSON Schema validated against the step output via AJV

equals.description: Deep-equal comparison. Strings compared verbatim; objects/arrays compared via JSON canonical form.

matches.description: Regex (string source, no flags) matched against the stringified output. Max 500 chars.

contains.description: Substring(s) that must appear in the stringified output. Array → all must be present.

on_fail.description: halt (default): step becomes error on assertion failure. warn: step passes but failure list is attached to stepResult.expectWarnings.

prompt.description: Prompt to send to the AI (supports {{templates}})

model.description: Model to use for the agent step

into.description: Variable name to store output in context

mcpAccess.description: When true, grants the agent step access to MCP tools registered on the bridge. Defaults to false for subprocess driver steps.

tools.description: Tool allowlist. When `sandbox` is true, enforced via --allowed-tools on the spawned claude -p.

sandbox.description: Opt-in tool sandbox: enforce the `tools` allowlist by running claude -p with --permission-mode dontAsk + --allowed-tools (drops --dangerously-skip-permissions). Default off. Pure pass-through with mcpAccess: when both are set, MCP tool names must be listed in `tools` or they will be blocked (no auto-injection).

disallowedTools.description: Deny rules applied via --disallowed-tools regardless of sandbox mode.

kind.description: When set to 'judge', the agent step emits a structured verdictReason and stores the verdict in the run log.

reviews.description: Step output key the judge reviews (its `into`, default 'agent_output'). Required for the judge→refine loop.

max_revisions.description: OPT-IN judge→refine loop. Max revise→re-judge cycles when the verdict is 'request_changes' (0/absent = augment-only, no loop). Requires kind:judge + reviews.

on_exhausted.description: When the revision budget is exhausted and the judge still requests changes: 'halt' (default) fails the run; 'proceed' continues with the last draft.

downshift.description: OPT-IN cost-aware routing (Phase 4). Ordered cheaper fallbacks tried when budget.usdMax is set and the remaining budget is too tight for the preferred driver/model. Each entry overrides driver and/or model. Absent → preferred model always used.

escalate.description: OPT-IN quality-aware escalation (dual of downshift). Ordered MORE-capable fallbacks. In a judge→refine loop (reviews + max_revisions>0), the Nth request_changes revision re-runs the reviewed step with escalate[N-1] instead of the base model — start local/cheap, escalate to a stronger model only when output fails judgment. Each entry overrides driver and/or model.

chain.description: Alias for nested recipe name or path

vars.description: Template variables passed into a nested recipe step

output.description: Output key for the nested recipe result

id.description: Optional group id — used as awaits target by later steps

awaits.description: Step IDs that must complete before this group starts

parallel.description: Run these steps concurrently. Array form: steps run in parallel. Object form: map-reduce — each item in `each` is bound to `as` and the steps array runs once per item.

chain.description: Alias for nested recipe name or path

vars.description: Template variables passed into a nested recipe step

output.description: Output key for the nested recipe result

oneOf.description: Map-reduce form — iterate over a collection and run steps once per item.

each.description: Template expression that resolves to an array of items to iterate over (e.g. '{{outputs.list}}').

as.description: Variable name each item is bound to inside the steps (default: 'item').

steps.description: Steps to run for each item in the collection.

chain.description: Alias for nested recipe name or path

vars.description: Template variables passed into a nested recipe step

output.description: Output key for the nested recipe result

expect.description: Optional assertions for mocked recipe tests

outputs.description: Expected output paths or keys recorded by the run

errorMessage.description: Expected final error message, or null for a clean run

context.description: Expected context key/value pairs after the run

retry.description: Number of retries per failing step (overridden by step.retry)

fallback.description: log_only / deliver_original: treat step failure as non-fatal (like optional); fail-open. abort (default): propagate failure.

notify.description: When false, suppresses Slack notifications on step failure even when a Slack connector is connected.

budget.description: Per-recipe budget. Set tokensMax and/or usdMax; on breach the run halts with budget_exceeded (or warns). Enforced for API drivers that report token usage and (for usdMax) have a price-table entry; subscription drivers (Claude CLI) report no tokens and are skipped (fail-open with a one-time warning).

tokensMax.description: Cumulative input + output tokens allowed across the whole run

usdMax.description: Cumulative USD allowed across the whole run, priced from token usage via the model price table. Unpriced models / subscription drivers fail open (never halt on them).

estimateUnmeasured.description: OPT-IN (default false). Estimate the notional list-price USD an unmeasured/subscription call would have cost and surface a ≈$ figure. Label only — never counted toward usdMax, never halts. Requires usdMax.

onBreach.description: halt (default): stop run on next admission check. warn: continue but record the breach in the run log. Applies to both tokensMax and usdMax.

servers.description: Plugin package specifiers (npm package names or file paths) whose tools are loaded and available to steps in this recipe. Loaded once at run start.
